description_long: This object is a horizontally oriented segment of interlocking stone blocks, arranged in three staggered rows. The blocks are rectangular, with slightly irregular edges and a rough, weathered surface texture, giving the appearance of natural stone. The coloration is a dark gray, with subtle variations and shading that suggest age and exposure to the elements. The ends of the segment are uneven, with some blocks protruding slightly, indicating it is part of a larger modular system. The surface is flat and broad, suitable for use as a barrier or low wall in an urban environment. There are no visible engravings, markings, or decorative elements, emphasizing its utilitarian function.
description: A dark gray, rough-textured segment of interlocking stone blocks arranged in staggered rows, forming a low, modular barrier. The surface is weathered and flat, with uneven ends and no visible markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the staggered arrangement of rectangular stone blocks and the uneven ends.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile and the layered stacking of the blocks.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the same staggered block pattern as the front, with visible surface texture.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness and the block layering.
attribute_location_context: urban retaining wall or barrier along a sidewalk or park edge
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface appears weathered with slight discoloration and roughness, suggesting exposure to outdoor elements. No significant moss or erosion is visible.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: interlocked with neighboring blocks to form part of a continuous wall
